concept.concept_text: A still-image slideshow following a 14th-century French apprentice stonemason as he learns his craft. The visuals favor composed tableaux that hold attention as the narrator carries the story.
concept.style_text: Painterly documentary realism: warm tungsten and northern window light, dust motes in beams, slate-grey and ochre palette. Vermeer-adjacent framing. Hands and tools take center stage.

characters.name: Pierre
characters.description: Young apprentice stonemason, ~16 years old, lean build, weathered linen tunic over wool hose, calloused hands, brown hair, hazel eyes.

characters.name: Master Henri
characters.description: Master mason in his fifties, grey-streaked beard, leather apron, measuring rod and chalk, patient demeanor.

chapters.name: Foundations
chapters.description: Pierre's first day at the lodge. Tools, names, foundation stones.
chapters.style_notes: Cool morning light, blue-grey shadows, hand-tool surfaces and chalk lines.

scenes.name: Opening — Cathedral Yard at Dawn
scenes.narration_text: Before stone could become cathedral, it had to become language — a vocabulary of cuts, of angles, of shadows learned by the hand long before the mind could name them.
scenes.image_prompt: Wide establishing shot of a medieval cathedral construction yard at dawn, stacked sandstone blocks under canvas tarpaulins, scaffolding rising into morning mist, a single lantern still burning by the master mason's stool. Cool blue-grey light, painterly composition. Natural exposure, deep shadows. No characters.
scenes.negative_prompt: modern construction equipment, electric lights, hi-vis clothing, glass facades
scenes.flow_idea: Open on the yard before the workers arrive. Hold on silence. Cold air, weight of the day.

scenes.name: Pierre Arrives
scenes.narration_text: Pierre came to the lodge at fourteen. He carried nothing of his own except a wool scarf his mother had woven for him the winter before, and a small leather pouch containing his entire inheritance: a single iron file.
scenes.image_prompt: Medium shot of Pierre, a young apprentice stonemason approaching the lodge entrance through an arched stone doorway. Weathered linen tunic over wool hose, small leather pouch in one hand. Soft side-light from a narrow window catches one side of his face. Background out of focus — work benches, hanging tools. Painterly composition, natural northern light.
scenes.negative_prompt: modern clothing, sunglasses, smiling at camera
scenes.flow_idea: Introduce Pierre. Frame him small in the doorway to convey arrival and uncertainty.

lyrics_source.initial_text:
Before stone could become cathedral, it had to become language — a vocabulary of cuts, of angles, of shadows learned by the hand long before the mind could name them.

Pierre came to the lodge at fourteen. He carried nothing of his own except a wool scarf his mother had woven for him the winter before, and a small leather pouch containing his entire inheritance: a single iron file.
